subject: Public Health
title: Zika and Emerging Viruses
description: A fast breakdown of Zika and Emerging Viruses for builders.

hook.text: In 2015, babies in Brazil were born with unusually small heads. Doctors traced it to a mosquito virus.

buildup.text: Zika virus, known since 1947, had never caused serious disease until it reached the Americas.

discovery.text: The virus crossed the placenta and attacked fetal brain cells, causing microcephaly.

twist.text: Zika was sexually transmitted too—the first mosquito-borne virus known to spread between humans this way.

climax.text: Climate change is expanding mosquito ranges, making future Zika-like outbreaks more likely worldwide.

punchline.text: Old viruses in new places create new dangers.

quiz.question: What birth defect did Zika virus cause in Brazil?
